events.body: US Army establishes Camp Huachuca during the Apache Wars. Becomes permanent fort in 1882.
events.title: Fort Huachuca Established

events.body: Named after Medal of Honor recipient Frank Luke Jr. Becomes primary fighter pilot training base.
events.title: Luke Field Activated

events.body: Activated for WWII bomber training. B-24 and B-29 crews trained here.
events.title: Davis-Monthan Becomes Army Air Field

events.body: First surplus military aircraft stored in the Arizona desert. Low humidity prevents corrosion.
events.title: Aircraft Storage Begins at D-M

events.body: U.S. Army Electronic Proving Ground established. Fort becomes center for military intelligence.
events.title: Fort Huachuca Army Intelligence

events.body: Aerospace Maintenance & Regeneration Group established at Davis-Monthan. Today holds 4,400+ aircraft.
events.title: AMARG Formalized

events.body: US Army Intelligence Center and School formally established.
events.title: Fort Huachuca Intelligence Center

events.body: US Army Network Enterprise Technology Command headquarters at Fort Huachuca.
events.title: NETCOM Activated

events.body: First F-35A Lightning II arrives at Luke. Base becomes primary F-35 training installation.
events.title: Luke AFB Gets F-35

events.body: Following the famous Washington state sighting, multiple Arizona reports emerge in July 1947.
events.title: Kenneth Arnold Sighting Aftermath

events.body: Alleged UFO retrieval near Kingman, AZ. Arthur Stansel testimony. No official confirmation.
events.title: Kingman UFO Crash Claim

events.body: Logger Travis Walton claims 5-day abduction near Snowflake, AZ. Coworker witnesses, polygraph tests. Became Fire in the Sky.
events.title: Travis Walton Incident

events.body: Mass sighting of V-shaped formation from Prescott to Tucson. Thousands of witnesses. Two distinct events: formation lights and flares.
events.title: The Phoenix Lights

events.body: Gov. Fife Symington holds mocking press conference with aide in alien costume. Later reverses, says he saw something unexplainable.
events.title: Governor Symington Press Conference

events.body: New lights reported over Phoenix on 10th anniversary. Later attributed to flares dropped by military.
events.title: Phoenix Lights Anniversary Sighting

events.body: DoD officially releases three Navy UAP videos. Renewed interest in Arizona military range sightings.
events.title: Pentagon UAP Video Release

events.body: David Grusch testimony mentions retrieval programs. Arizona military facilities referenced in broader UAP discourse.
events.title: Congressional UAP Hearings

events.body: Seven states divide Colorado River water. Arizona initially refuses to sign, doesn't ratify until 1944.
events.title: Colorado River Compact

events.body: Supreme Court rules in Arizona's favor, establishing water allocation rights.
events.title: Arizona v. California

events.body: Colorado River Basin Project Act authorizes the Central Arizona Project — a 336-mile canal.
events.title: CAP Authorized

events.body: First water flows through the CAP canal system to Phoenix metro area.
events.title: CAP Begins Deliveries

events.body: First-ever shortage declaration on the Colorado River. Arizona forced to cut water use by 512,000 acre-feet.
events.title: Tier 1 Shortage Declared

events.body: Additional reductions. Lake Mead at historic lows. Arizona agriculture faces existential pressure.
events.title: Tier 2 Cuts

events.body: States begin negotiating new Colorado River operating guidelines to replace 2007 interim rules.
events.title: Post-2026 Negotiations Begin
